whatDone: ERP decision forum completed on 2026-02-26 with team alignment to proceed with NetSuite; selection phase moved to post-selection execution planning.
Finance stakeholder feedback captured from demos (P2P depth concerns, workload concerns, approval workflow gaps).
P2P identified as one of the three canonical ERP program priorities.
Initial follow-up sent to KPMG focused on P2P detail / what must be seen and clarified.
France and US internal P2P fact-finding emails sent to Trinidad, Kimberly, and Matt requesting existing process documentation first.

Reviewed NetSuite Demonstration Feedback deck (source artifact) confirming concrete P2P/enablement questions: Trustpair integration proof points and detailed interface classification (custom vs bundled vs paid app).

Reviewed SAP Demonstration Feedback deck (source artifact) confirming additional P2P/finance concerns: dedicated SAP BI reporting session request for P2P/finance and proposal scope/RACI clarifications (interfaces, forms, workflows).

CFTI SOX implementation call confirmed finalized P2P RCM and remediation tracker exist, with ERP-related controls/gaps already identified and at least one vendor-management gap tied to Sage material weakness.
Received CFTI attachments from Youness (Project Plan tracker workbook, FSCP RCM, P2P RCM) and confirmed workbook/tab structures via local OpenXML inspection.

Received and parsed Trinidad France P2P artifact bundle (10 files) into the system: 2 approval matrices, 2 P2P SOP/procedure PDFs, signature policy, currency-entry guide, DocuShare user guide, supplier master-cleaning workbook, and payment-process workbook.

Trinidad payment-process workbook intake confirms operational details useful for process mapping, including Trustpair/Agicap/Sage references, an AGICAP sheet, an EBICS signer-key mention, a documented cadence of two vendor payment campaigns per month (10th and 25th), and a payment-file preparation lead-time note (~4 working days before value date).
Trinidad payment-process workbook intake references Juliette and Fatma in payment-file preparation roles, which gives named-owner clues for France payment-step mapping and walkthrough targeting.

Trinidad supplier-data workbook intake confirms vendor-master data fields/cleanup guidance are documented (including country, VAT, Siret, and bank fields) plus identification/bank/free-field setup tabs, which supports vendor-setup current-state mapping.
Juliette walkthrough notes confirm the real France AP control layer is DocuShare approval email plus Juliette's master Excel tracker, not Sage workflow.
Juliette walkthrough notes confirm current PO approval logic is amount-only today; future-state requirement is threshold plus department or cost center routing.

Juliette walkthrough notes confirm invoice-to-PO checking happens in the spreadsheet, invoice-over-PO exceptions are handled manually with some leeway, and future-state should include configurable tolerance thresholds.
Juliette walkthrough notes confirm only Juliette and Fatma create vendors in Sage, Juliette enters bank information there, and Trustpair checks banking data twice per day.
Juliette walkthrough notes confirm there is no formal aging report and effectively no usable P2P reporting across France today.
openItems: Current-state P2P workflow map by entity (SA vs LLC) is not yet documented in the program baseline.
Must-have P2P controls/workflows list is not yet formalized for implementation scope.
1099-specific process requirements remain unclear.
Need proof points and implementation examples for Trustpair integration feasibility (explicitly flagged in NetSuite demo feedback).
Need vendor response separating interfaces into custom build vs standard app vs paid app to avoid hidden scope/cost.
Need side-by-side SAP vs NetSuite evidence on P2P integrations/interfaces/workflows to avoid decision-making from demo style rather than implementation scope reality.
Need CFTI P2P RCM intake to identify which of the ERP-related controls/gaps should be explicit ERP design requirements vs non-ERP remediation.
Need PDF-level extraction/review of Trinidad approval matrices/SOPs/signature/currency docs to complement the workbook-derived operational signals.
Need explicit design decision on France invoice-over-PO tolerance policy: percent threshold, absolute amount threshold, approver path, and hard-stop behavior.
waitingOn: KPMG response on P2P-focused follow-up and available process/detail documentation.
Kimberly/Matt response with US LLC P2P process artifacts and current review/evidence practices.
Detailed row-level extraction/classification of the CFTI P2P RCM and project-plan micro view (files received; structure confirmed).
nextMoves: Review parsed Trinidad bundle summaries and priority artifacts (approval matrices, SOP/procedures, payment workbook, DocuShare guide) and convert them into a source-backed France P2P current-state map before the next walkthrough.
Deep-dive Trinidad payment workbook tabs to map current payment sequencing, Trustpair touchpoints, Agicap handoffs, and signer/validator roles.
Review France and US artifacts before live sessions to keep meetings focused and reduce generic discussion.
Build side-by-side France vs US current-state P2P map (steps, approvals, exceptions, evidence).
Convert findings into a P2P must-have workflow/control list tied to ERP design checkpoints.
Map CFTI P2P RCM gaps into three buckets: ERP-addressable, immediate non-ERP remediation, and management/ownership decisions.

Turn Juliette's France notes into explicit design requirements for spreadsheet replacement, approval routing by threshold plus cost center, tolerance thresholds, and native aging or exception reporting.

evidenceRequests.topic: P2P process detail and what must be seen in ERP solution fit
evidenceRequests.why: Moves P2P pillar from generic preference comments to specific workflow, approval, and control requirements.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Use response to define P2P must-haves and map into selection/implementation scope.

evidenceRequests.topic: France P2P current-state documentation and must-have ERP process changes
evidenceRequests.why: Starts internal fact-finding on the France-side P2P process, controls, workarounds, and audit-trail gaps using existing documentation first.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Use the parsed Trinidad bundle inventory (approval matrices, SOPs, payment workbook, vendor-master workbook, DocuShare guide) to build a focused walkthrough agenda tied to documented steps, approvals, and evidence gaps.

evidenceRequests.topic: US LLC P2P current-state documentation and must-have ERP process changes
evidenceRequests.why: Builds US-side P2P baseline (vendor setup, payment/review/posting, audit evidence, exceptions) before design assumptions are made.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Compare US and France P2P flows and identify where a global design can be standardized vs intentionally different by entity.

evidenceRequests.topic: Finalized P2P RCM and remediation tracker (ERP-related controls/gaps)
evidenceRequests.why: Provides control-by-control evidence for P2P design requirements and immediate remediation opportunities.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Extract P2P RCM control rows/gap remediation fields and classify ERP-addressable vs non-ERP vs management-decision items.

whatDone: Hema identified Robin Lapous (KPMG) as the contact supporting consolidation and IFRS adjustments; process currently described as manual in Excel with no formal documentation/process flows.
US Finance intro notes captured manual SEC reporting / IFRS bridge dependency and Sage extract limitations.
Reporting/Data identified as one of the three canonical ERP program priorities.
Living executive deck scaffold includes reporting baseline and bridge dependency as a high-priority data request.
Reviewed NetSuite Demonstration Feedback deck for proposal/workload assumptions; confirms need to validate scope/resourcing assumptions before relying on reporting capability claims.
Frederick fact-finding call captured pragmatic reporting target: improve quarterly reporting quality/visibility first; monthly reporting is a later-stage need (likely 2027/2028).

Frederick described Workday Adaptive/Interactive as an underused transition tool intended for internal consolidation/intercompany support and forecasting/variance visibility, currently used at low capacity.
Reporting/Data pillar now includes explicit cross-pillar dependency: P2P process timing quality affects reporting quality and timing.

CFTI SOX implementation call identified finalized FSCP RCM and remediation tracker, with functional ownership pointers (Trinidad/Hema for FSCP, Frederick for FP&A, Roxandra for Treasury, Sophie for HR) that can help structure reporting/controls owner mapping.
Received CFTI FSCP RCM and project-plan tracker workbooks from Youness and confirmed sheet/header structures (including reporting-close control fields and remediation-plan column).
Sent follow-up to Hema requesting the correct non-ERP KPMG contacts for reporting/process advisory (bridge/consolidation/current-state documentation owners).
openItems: Current consolidation/IFRS adjustment process is manual in Excel with limited/no formal documentation; need direct KPMG fact-finding with Robin Lapous to map current-state flow and dependencies.
Current reporting process baseline (French GAAP -> IFRS -> SEC) is not documented in one source in the app.
COA/cost center future-state design principles (global consistency vs entity separation) are not yet formalized.
Internal vs KPMG ownership split by reporting step is not yet mapped.
Frederick note: current system lacks a country tag, which appears important for reporting and cost center design/use cases.
Reporting design should prioritize a scalable quarterly operating model first, with monthly capability as future-ready rather than immediate scope.
Current milestone billing is not automated, creating reporting/timing visibility limitations.
Commercial/licensing/royalty reporting requirements exist as future design considerations (2027/2028) but are not core ERP scope at this stage.
Department reporting expectations are not standardized today (e.g., clinical visibility expectations unclear).
Need FSCP RCM intake to identify reporting/financial statement close control gaps that should shape near-term data model and process design decisions.
waitingOn: Hema response / source docs for current reporting process and bridge workflow.
Frederick response with reporting dimensions/core outputs documentation (following 2026-02-24 follow-up email).
Follow-up detail from Matt / Adrian to confirm owners, timing, and data pain points for current reporting outputs.
Detailed row-level extraction/classification of FSCP RCM and tracker micro-view rows (files received; structure confirmed).
nextMoves: Build reporting baseline flow (steps, owners, systems, KPMG handoffs).
Tag which reporting pain points are ERP-addressable vs process/governance-only.
Define a minimum reporting dimension set (including country/entity/cost center and other Frederick-required dimensions) for core ERP/data design.
Separate near-term reporting needs (quarterly quality/visibility) from later-phase scalability needs (monthly/commercial/royalties) in scope discussions.
Use FSCP RCM to anchor reporting/close control requirements and owner assignments to actual control IDs/gaps.

evidenceRequests.topic: Current reporting process docs (French GAAP -> IFRS / SEC bridge, owners, steps, internal vs KPMG split)
evidenceRequests.why: Creates the baseline for the Reporting/Data pillar and identifies what can move in-house vs remain external.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Convert response into current-state reporting flow and target-state ERP reporting requirements.

evidenceRequests.topic: Identify non-ERP KPMG contacts for reporting / bridge / consolidation process fact-finding
evidenceRequests.why: Separates ERP-selection KPMG contacts (Camille/Aymen) from other KPMG advisory teams that may already own reporting-process documentation and bridge/consolidation workpapers.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Reach out to Robin Lapous (KPMG) on current consolidation / IFRS adjustments process and identify what documentation or workpapers can be used for reporting process mapping.

evidenceRequests.topic: FP&A reporting dimensions and core output needs
evidenceRequests.why: Anchors reporting/data design to actual dimension and output needs (not generic future-state reporting assumptions).
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Use Frederick inputs to define minimum reporting dimensions and core quarterly output requirements in the reporting-data baseline.

evidenceRequests.topic: Finalized FSCP RCM and remediation tracker (financial close/reporting control gaps)
evidenceRequests.why: Adds documented FSCP control gaps and ownership to the Reporting/Data pillar baseline and ties reporting design to real deficiencies.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Extract FSCP RCM control rows and map gaps/remediation plans to reporting flow steps, owners, and ERP/non-ERP categories.

whatDone: Controls/Auditability identified as a core ERP pillar and reflected in board/audit deck scaffolds.
IT and Finance intro notes captured control-relevant issues (manual flows, weak audit trail, inconsistent evidence).
Hema and Jade requests sent to obtain documented SOX findings, remediation tracking, and IT control documentation.
Reviewed Jade IT Master Plan 2026-2027 deck (source artifact) confirming compliance scope explicitly includes SOX compliance, change request management, CSV/IQ, audits, and inspection readiness.
Reviewed SAP Demonstration Feedback deck showing proposal/RACI scrutiny and explicit concern on governance/PMO sufficiency (control-relevant for delivery oversight).

CFTI SOX implementation call confirmed finalized P2P and FSCP RCMs, an active remediation tracker (macro/micro views with control IDs and statuses), ERP-related design gaps, non-ERP immediate remediation opportunities, and management/ownership decision gaps.
Received Youness CFTI attachment package and confirmed workbook structures: project tracker macro/micro tabs plus finalized P2P and FSCP RCM tabs with control metadata/remediation columns.
openItems: Documented SOX findings / deficiencies are not yet loaded into the program baseline.
Finance vs IT remediation ownership and tracking process is not yet confirmed in one place.
ERP-addressable vs non-ERP control remediation split has not yet been mapped.
Documented control deficiencies/findings are still missing even though IT roadmap confirms SOX/compliance workstreams exist.
ITGC RCM is not yet finalized (expected next week), so full application + IT control view is still incomplete.
Need explicit prioritization of the ~8 ERP-related controls in P2P/FSCP for ERP blueprint sequencing and early remediation planning.
waitingOn: Hema response with SOX findings, remediation tracker location, and control narratives.
Jade response with IT control gaps, interface monitoring/failure handling docs, and admin ownership matrix.
Row-level extraction of P2P/FSCP RCM and project tracker micro-view to convert workbook content into app issue/control mappings.
Walid/Youness (CFTI) follow-up next week for ITGC RCM after final validation.
nextMoves: Create deficiency inventory and tag each item as ERP-addressable / process / governance / audit-only.
Use findings as the basis for controls design checkpoints in ERP blueprint and audit committee reporting.
Use Jade IT Master Plan roadmap as supporting context, but anchor controls pillar to actual findings/deficiencies once Hema/Jade responses arrive.
Use CFTI tracker structure (macro/micro tabs) as the baseline issue register format for ERP controls remediation mapping and status reporting.
Track French e-invoicing September legal change as a control/process design dependency that may affect AP/P2P control design.

evidenceRequests.topic: Documented SOX findings / deficiencies, remediation tracker, and control narratives
evidenceRequests.why: Gives the Controls pillar a documented problem list so ERP design can target real deficiencies.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Tag deficiencies that ERP can address directly vs process/governance-only remediation.

evidenceRequests.topic: IT control gaps, interface monitoring/failure handling docs, and system admin ownership matrix
evidenceRequests.why: Adds IT control evidence and ownership paths needed for finance-system auditability and SOX support.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Map IT controls into ERP controls design and enablement dependencies.

evidenceRequests.topic: Finalized P2P/FSCP RCMs and remediation tracker (macro/micro status views with control IDs)
evidenceRequests.why: Provides the documented deficiency/gap list and remediation tracker needed to make the Controls pillar evidence-based and audit-ready.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Extract tracker/RCM rows and classify control gaps into ERP-addressable, immediate non-ERP, and management-decision buckets; then use in audit committee reporting.

evidenceRequests.topic: ITGC RCM (awaiting final validation; expected next week)
evidenceRequests.why: Completes the controls baseline with ITGC coverage beyond P2P/FSCP process controls.
evidenceRequests.nextMove: Follow up next week once final validation is complete and integrate ITGC gaps into controls pillar mapping.

whatDone: NetSuite selected on 2026-02-26; program moves into post-selection communication, negotiation, and implementation readiness planning.
IT landscape and architecture context captured (systems, integrations, hosting, security tooling, ownership hints).
Access request tracking and SharePoint evidence intake are active in the app.
Program and system map pages now expose cross-cutting dependencies and automation status.

Reviewed Jade IT Master Plan 2026-2027 deck confirming IT org reporting line (Didier -> Christophe -> Jade/Benjamin/Erwan) and roadmap alignment to ERP framing/build, finance integrations, SOX, and filing milestones.
IT roadmap source evidence now confirms ERP is embedded within broader IT priorities (cybersecurity, compliance, business applications) rather than a standalone project.
openItems: Need explicit post-selection next-step plan with Camille/KPMG and NetSuite covering notification, negotiation path, ownership, and early-April mobilization target.
Direct SharePoint Graph automation is not enabled (permission blocker).
System owner/admin matrix is still incomplete for several finance applications.
Internal capacity commitments by function/month are not yet documented.
Need the updated Sage-centric IT map and centralized interface inventory to convert roadmap context into implementation-level control/process evidence.
waitingOn: Jade/Finance responses and source docs for ownership matrices and interface monitoring details.
IT/admin approval path if direct SharePoint Graph permissions are pursued.
nextMoves: Draft/align post-selection next-step email to Camille (cc Hema) to lock owners, negotiation path, and timing toward early April work start.
Continue using fallback email-based evidence intake while preparing for SharePoint Graph activation.
Map enablement blockers explicitly to pillar-level delivery risk in executive reporting.
